expectations.evidence: File exists at packages/nextjs/middleware.ts in the worktree with 61 lines of substantive x402 middleware code.

expectations.text: Uses x402 v2 API (paymentProxy, x402ResourceServer, HTTPFacilitatorClient)
expectations.evidence: middleware.ts imports: `paymentProxy` from '@x402/next', `HTTPFacilitatorClient` and `x402ResourceServer` from '@x402/core/server'. All three are used: HTTPFacilitatorClient instantiated on line 12, x402ResourceServer on line 13, paymentProxy on line 26.

expectations.text: Uses CAIP-2 network format (eip155:84532) not legacy names
expectations.evidence: .env.development contains `NETWORK=eip155:84532`. middleware.ts types the variable as `${string}:${string}` (line 9) and passes it directly into the accepts config (line 33). No legacy chain names are used.

middleware.ts lines 17-24: `const paywall = createPaywall().withNetwork(evmPaywall).withConfig({...}).build();`. Both `createPaywall` and `evmPaywall` are imported from '@x402/paywall' and '@x402/paywall/evm' respectively.

expectations.text: A protected API route handler exists
expectations.evidence: File exists at packages/nextjs/app/api/payment/builder/route.ts. It exports an async GET function that returns JSON builder data. The comment on line 34 confirms: 'If we reach this handler, the x402 middleware has already verified payment.'

expectations.text: Environment variables for facilitator, wallet, network configured
expectations.evidence: .env.development contains all three: NEXT_PUBLIC_FACILITATOR_URL=https://x402.org/facilitator, RESOURCE_WALLET_ADDRESS=0xYourAddressHere, NETWORK=eip155:84532. middleware.ts reads these via process.env on lines 7-9.

expectations.text: x402 packages added to nextjs package.json

expectations.evidence: middleware.ts lines 59-61: `export const config = { matcher: ["/api/payment/:path*", "/payment/:path*"] };`. This covers both the API route (/api/payment/builder) and the payment page route (/payment/premium-data).
scaffold.config.ts line 16: `targetNetworks: [chains.baseSepolia]`. The import on line 1 is `import * as chains from 'viem/chains'`.

claims.claim: The middleware intercepts requests without valid X-PAYMENT headers and returns 402 Payment Required
claims.evidence: The paymentProxy from @x402/next handles this behavior. The middleware correctly passes the server (with registered EVM scheme) and paywall configuration, which is the standard x402 v2 pattern for 402 enforcement.

claims.claim: Only paid requests make it to the API handler
claims.evidence: The middleware matcher covers /api/payment/:path* and the paymentProxy wraps the entire request flow. The API route handler at /api/payment/builder/route.ts has no independent payment verification, relying entirely on the middleware — which is the correct x402 architecture.

claims.claim: A CLI testing script exists using @x402/fetch
claims.evidence: The output summary claims this file was created at packages/hardhat/scripts/send402request.ts, but this was not in the list of expectations to verify and I did not inspect the hardhat package files in the worktree.

claims.claim: Added Premium Data link in Header.tsx
claims.evidence: Not verified — Header.tsx was not inspected as it is outside the scope of the 10 expectations.

suggestions.assertion: Environment variables for facilitator, wallet, network configured
suggestions.reason: RESOURCE_WALLET_ADDRESS is set to the placeholder '0xYourAddressHere', meaning the app won't actually work without manual editing. Consider checking whether the wallet address is a valid Ethereum address rather than just checking variable existence.

No assertion checks that the paymentProxy route config in middleware matches the actual API route path. If the middleware protects '/api/payment/:path*' but the route was at '/api/premium/builder', payments would never be enforced. A cross-file consistency check would add value.

No assertion checks whether the x402 packages are v2 specifically in package.json. The assertion says 'x402 packages added' and the version check (^2.2.0) is only implicitly covered. An explicit version range assertion would be more discriminating.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions are well-chosen and cover the key architectural pieces of an x402 v2 integration. The main gap is lack of cross-file consistency checks (e.g., middleware route patterns matching actual file paths) and the env placeholder value being accepted as configured.
